cooperates.cooperate_desc: Cyclops' ultimate can lock down enemy heroes and allows Masha to get close and burst them down, Cyclops' passive also allows him to spam his ultimate frequently which helps this synergy burst down enemies more frequently.
Angela can help Masha when she's outnumbered by enemies and allows her to stay in a fight longer.
Johnson can synergize with almost every hero in the game and Masha is no stranger to this. Masha can ride Johnson and Johnson can lock-down a target or multiple targets and let Masha burst them down.

counters.counter_desc: Sun's kit overwhelms Masha as Sun can inflict huge damage with his clones and burst down Masha. Masha will also have a hard time since Sun can create clones which makes it hard to distinguish which ones which.

Masha needs to be level 4 to go toe-to-toe with Yu Zhong, Masha is one of the heroes that can shut down Yu Zhong and burst him down easily even on his dragon form as Masha's huge damage overwhelms Yu Zhong's passive and Masha's passive let's her remove all debuffs when she loses an HP bar.

Masha can zone out Argus in the early game, but Masha easily gets countered after Argus reaches level 4, it's best to play safe or wait until his ultimate duration ends or after he used it to burst him down.

Guinevere's kit heavily relies on stunning the enemies, but Masha's unique passive effect when losing her HP bar removes debuffs and she receives a brief CC immune effect which allows Masha to burst down an out-of-position Guinevere too often.

dominants.dominant_desc: After reaching level 4, this is where Masha becomes scary, as her ultimate is now learnt, even without her core items, Masha can already inflict huge damage on heroes, which allows her to snowball. But don't overestimate Masha's capabilities, mid-game is where enemies gank and rotate to a lane faster and Masha can't handle multiple enemies at once, try to play safely and only engage when your team mates are around or when you've confirmed that the enemies aren't around to help your target.

Late-game is where Masha's effectiveness decreases but she's still scary in this phase of the game since one or two of her core items are already built. Team-fights are crucial in this phase as one wrong slip-up costs you the game, in situations like this, always target the ADC or the backline of the opposing side and secure the kill it doesn't matter if you die or not, your main goal in this phase is to kill the opponent's jungler or ADC, if you managed to survive the team fight it is already a bonus, in which you can take down their mage or even their fighter.

emblems.emblem_desc: This is the go to emblem set for Masha, Rupture gives her an early physical penetration increase allowing her to deal consistent damage early on, Master Assasin is good for ambushing enemies and for out-of-position enemies that can be bursted down and Lethal Ignition gives her a damage increase that allows her to deal massive damage.

equips.equip_desc:
Sea Halberd is one of Masha's core items, the lifebane effect of the item allows her to burst down or 1v1 heroes that relies on spell vamp and lifesteal.

Blade of the Heptaseas and Endless Battle synergizes well with her first skill that enhances her basic attack damage, the true damage effect both skill gives is great, as Masha's damage mostly comes from her basic attack.

Blade of Despair's passive increases Masha's effectiveness to secure kills and grant her a massive damage increase.

The last item is situational, but Malefic Roar is suited for her, the passive and Physical Penetration of the item negates physical defense items and allows her to retain her burst damage, especially in the late game where heroes are durable than ever.

This alternate build can be used by Masha if the enemy's composition is mostly durable heroes that can tank her damage due to having HP or sustainability.

Demon Hunter Sword's passive is a game changer that allows her to burst down high HP heroes and synergizes well with her since most of her damage comes from her basic attack.

Hunter Strike is a good replacement for Blade of the Heptaseas for mobility and both give the same Physical Penetration stat that synergizes well with Malefic Roar.

hero.hero_overview: Masha is one of the unique heroes in MLBB you can encounter or play. She's the only hero that has 3 HP bars in the game and her ability to inflict huge damage on enemies allows her to snipe down or hunt squishy heroes or the enemy's ADC (Attack Damage Carry)
hero.hero_strength: Masha excels in the mid-game when her core items have been built or when she reaches level 4. Masha shines in 1v1 combat and can easily burst down squishy heroes. Masha is also a good zoner in turtle or lord fights as she can hunt down or ambush enemy junglers and give your team an advantage when you managed to kill or made the opposing jungler retreat.
hero.hero_weakness: While dealing massive damage, the trade back is that her skills require HP to be casted (Doesn't apply for her last HP bar) and leaves her vulnerable if she's out-of-position. Her effectiveness also dwindles in team fights where enemies are close to each other.

hero.hero_overview:
Masha is a durable fighter/tank hero in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang, known for her high sustainability, multiple health bars , and strong split-pushing capabilities .Here's an overview of her key attributes:
 

Masha - Wild-Oath Keeper

Role: Fighter/Tank
Specialty: Regen / Push
Lane: EXP Lane


Hero Traits:

 Three HP Bars: Masha has three separate health bars, making her extremely difficult to burst down. Each bar needs to be depleted individually.
 Energy-based Hero: Instead of mana, Masha uses energy for her skills, allowing her to spam abilities without running out of resources.
 High Sustain: Her passive allows her to regenerate HP quickly when out of combat.
Objective Pressure: Masha excels at taking down turrets and even Lord/Turtle with high attack speed and damage.

---

Skills:

1. Passive
   Grants 3 HP bars. She gains attack speed as she loses HP and regenerates HP when one of her HP bars is lost.

2. Skill 1
   Boosts movement speed and increases basic attack damage based on enemy max HP, at the cost of energy.

3. Skill 2 
   Deals damage and silences enemies briefly, useful for disrupting enemy skills.

4. Ultimate

hero.hero_overview: Masha is a very strong and somewhat forgotten heroine, if you know how to use her correctly, she will be a powerful weapon in your hands and you will be able to raise your ranking easily. Certainly, the best roles for Masha are in the experience and jungle paths, and they generally depend on what your team needs. I will explain the two paths based on my experience. First, in the experience path: Buy the normal boots first so that you have reasonable speed and try to farm and take the last hit for each minion, and also try to take the crab so that you have an advantage over your opponent and reach level 4 as quickly as possible, then go and help your team take the turtle. Try as much as possible not to enter team confrontations without your ultimate skill , . As for the jungle: Try to take the buff close to the gold path so that you can reach the experience path and help the fighter reach level 4 quickly and help you take the turtle.  Tell the tanker in your team to help you with the farm and do not intervene quickly until you get your first or second item, so that you can then launch the attack with your team, as Masha has a lot of life points and great damage with items, so you will be complete if you get your items quickly, and of course you must be wise in the game.
hero.hero_strength: In the early Try to farm a lot in the first of the game so you can reach level 4 quickly, and you dont waste your hp for nothing . Be always wise and you will make great results. In mid game be with your team and your essential target is the mm and the magical, you can also target the tank or the fighter if there is no finisher around your enemy.if you have enough hp you can enter the turret and finish your enemy. In the late game you will have brutial damege especially if you use full damege emblem ,but the enemy's heroes will be strong and won't be easy to kill . So I advice you to target just the main effected heroes and buy the immortality shield so you you can recharge your hp by pressing your third skill fast .

hero.hero_overview: Masha is a strong late game hero with insane burst damage and finishing abilities. She can either be in exp or roam, Masha is a fighter/tank role designed for single targets and can be a potential threat to any squishy heroes. Masha is weak in the early game, the key for her survival is to maintain her passive on a fair scale, once she is down to her last round of HP she is prone to other burst heroes that can one-hit her, do note that in full health, she is immune to it. Masha's skills come at a cost of her HP so be wise when you use her. Also, her last skill is the main course of her combo, everytime one of her health gets depleted the cool down of her third skill will be reset, fun right?
hero.hero_strength: In the start of the game, Masha cannot do much other than hitting minions on the first wave, so it's best to play safe. When in roam, try to help as much as you can in the gold lane, and you cannot leave your marksman yet without it reaching level four, and try to buy dire hit in this situation. Once your marksman had gained an advantage, try to migrate to the other lane to support other allies, when in turtle fights, try not to engage too much until you have sea helbierg, and only then you can join in on the clashing. Remember, it is crucial for Masha to gain equipment in mid game because her skills and damage mostly rely on accessories:) on the other hand, when you are in exp, in level one try to get her second skill first, this helps Masha to poke and last hit minions as she is weak on the early game. When it comes to equipment, prioritize buying sea helbierg to disrupt healing abilities on her enemy, after that straight to buying her second core item Which is the malefic gun to add explosion damage to her third skill, only then you can now buy rapid boots. Remember, Masha does NOT need any defense items now, so try to make her deadly as possible. Her only capability is to finish and kill heroes with ease.
hero.hero_weakness: Masha might be strong, but be careful as there are a few threats to herself if you are blind. Try to avoid heroes with vengeance, as it can bring her damage back at her and break her claws, it is wise to wait until the effect has worn out then you can continue strangling your victims. The same goes for wind chant, this item are annoying to any Masha's, because it cannot allow you to damage your enemies once it is triggered. The only way to done it is to simply strategize, use petrification while you have, this allows you to freeze your opponents, just enough to get you some time until the effects will wear off. Remember, try to avoid defense items, I mean, it is okay to buy it but I don't think it is necessary though as she have three health bars and in order to trigger her third skill again you have to be able to get rid of that phase so then you can continue delivering more continuous damage to the enemy, buying defenses however could help her survivability but at the same time, it can give her enemy enough time to escape via flicker, sprint, or even wind chant. Her counter heroes are argus, sun, Julian, belerick, and odette. These heroes can give continues damage to Masha while getting attacked by her. Odette with winter crown is enough to drain Masha's extra health to zero. Argus has potential, he can outlast Masha's series of punches without being scathed. Sun can be unfair, three against one is not to be messed with. Belerick is tanky enough to deal with her, and Julian can easily escape her at the right moment. So whenever you want to try Masha, just remember to ban these heroes or not.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Masha and Harith make an excellent duo, with Masha being a solid frontline tank and Harith providing high mobility and burst damage. Their complementary roles allow them to cover each other's weaknesses while maximizing their strengths in team fights.

Masha and Yu Zhong make a strong synergy due to their combined durability, sustain, and damage output. Masha can initiate fights and absorb damage, allowing Yu Zhong to follow up with his sustained damage. However, the lack of strong crowd control and their somewhat overlapping roles might make this combination less effective against certain team compositions.

Masha's tankiness allows her to dive into enemy lines while Zhuxin's control abilities keep enemies from escaping or retaliating, allowing Masha to stay on target. Zhuxin's burst damage also supports Masha's sustained assault, ensuring that they can take down enemies efficiently.

Masha and Johnson have an excellent synergy, with Johnson's ability to initate fights and Masha's tankiness and sustained damage complementing each other perfectly. Their coordination allows them to control fights and apply pressure to enemies effectively, making them a excellent duo.

counters.counter_desc: Belerick is a strong counter to Masha, especially in prolonged team fights where his sustain and crowd control can limit Masha's ability to freely dish out damage. However, Masha's true damage and aggressive playstyle can still give her a chance if she can close the gap and land clean attacks. Belerick is a strong counter to Masha, especially in prolonged team fights where his sustain and crowd control can limit Masha's ability to freely dish out damage. However, Masha's true damage and aggressive playstyle can still give her a chance if she can close the gap and land clean attacks. The fight's outcome largely depends on positioning and timing, but Belerick's abilities give him an edge in a direct face-off.

Dyrroth counters Masha quite effectively due to his true damage, lifesteal, and burst damage that can bypass her tankiness. While Masha's sustain gives her some chance in prolonged fights, Dyrroth's overall kit provides him an advantage in duels. The fight outcome depends on the situation, positioning, and timing, but Dyrroth generally has the upper hand in direct confrontations

Diggie can be an annoying counter to Masha due to his crowd control immunity, which renders Masha's attempts to disable and lock down enemies ineffective. His Time's Journey can also disrupt Masha's ability to engage properly, and his Auto Alarm Bomb further hinders her mobility. However, Masha's lifesteal and true damage allow her to keep fighting through the disruptions if she can close the gap and survive long enough. Overall, Diggie can make life difficult for Masha, but with the right positioning and timing, Masha still has a fighting chance.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Laning Tips: Focus on last-hitting minions and getting gold to build your core items. Use your lifesteal from Sea Halberd to sustain yourself in lane. Avoid extended trades with high-burst heroes and poke when they use abilities.

Precautions: Be cautious of enemy ganks, especially in side lanes. Keep an eye on the minimap and use Flicker to escape if needed.

Tips: Go aggressive if the enemy is overextended. Use basic attacks and abilities to harass them and push them back.

Laning Tips: After hitting level 4, Masha becomes stronger. Look to take down turrets and rotate to help other lanes. Aim to secure objectives like Turtle and Lord.

Precautions: Be aware of enemy jungler movements. Stay with your team when engaging in objectives to avoid being caught out alone.

Tips: Roam to other lanes for team fights and objectives. Use your mobility to secure kills and assists.

Team Fight Strategy: Position yourself to deal damage safely while avoiding getting caught. Focus on squishy targets first and use lifesteal to stay alive.

Precautions: Don't dive too deep into enemy lines. Rely on teammates to peel for you. Wait for the right moment to engage when your team is in a strong position.

Tips: Dive the backline when possible, using speed boosts from Endless Battle or Hunter Strike to chase down enemies. Use lifesteal to sustain yourself in prolonged fights.

emblems.emblem_desc:
I chose this Assassin Emblem set for Masha to enhance her offensive capabilities and increase her mobility. The +14 Adaptive Penetration and +10 Adaptive Attack provide a significant boost to her damage, allowing her to deal more damage to both tanky and squishy enemies. The +3% Movement Speed is crucial for improving her chasing potential, making it easier for her to close the gap on fleeing enemies or escape when needed.

Thrill helps further amplify her damage by increasing her Adaptive Attack, especially during intense fights. The Bargain Hunter talent is useful for maximizing Masha's gold efficiency, allowing her to buy items at a reduced price and improve her item progression faster. Lastly, Lethal Ignition synergizes well with Masha's burst damage, providing additional damage over time when dealing significant damage to enemies, ensuring she can finish off opponents more effectively in team fights. This Emblem set enhances both her damage output and survivability in fights, making her a strong threat in various situations.

equips.equip_desc:
Explanation for the First Build Choice:

This build focuses on maximizing Masha's damage while ensuring she has sustain and mobility. It balances offensive power with survivability, making her a deadly presence in both early and late game. The build prioritizes penetration, burs, and mobility, allowing her to handle tanky and squishy enemies effectively.

Key Equipment in the First Build:

1. Boots (Dire Hit) - Before buying Sea Halberd, buy boots first with the Dire Hit blessing. These boots provide Masha with increased movement speed and crit damage early in the game, allowing her to roam effectively and deal more damage in skirmishes.

2. Sea Halberd - After buying boots, the next priority is Sea Halberd. It provides physical penetration, crit chance, and lifesteal. The lifesteal allows Masha to sustain herself in fights and lane longer, while the penetration ensures she deals effective damage to tanky heroes.

3. Malefic Roar - Next, buy Malefic Roar for physical penetration. This item is essential for dealing with enemies who build armor, allowing Masha to shred through tanky heroes in team fights and individual skirmishes.

4. Blade of the Heptaseas - Blade of the Heptaseas increases Masha's damage against enemies with high HP by offering physical attack and penetration. It is a great item for dealing sustained damage to tanky or bruiser-type heroes.

 5. Blade of Despair - Blade of Despair gives Masha a massive power spike in her physical attack. It is especially effective against low-health enemies, allowing Masha to burst down squishier targets in team fights.

6. Hunter Strike - Hunter Strike provides cooldown reduction and a speed-up effect, making Masha more mobile in fights. The speed boost helps her chase down enemies and reposition quickly, while the cooldown reduction allows her to use her abilities more often.

7. Rapid Boots - Increases her speed and crit damage for better mobility and late-game impact.

Explanation of the Second Build Choice:

This build offers Masha excellent sustain, damage, and mobility. By replacing Hunter Strike with Endless Battle, Masha gains more lifesteal, damage, and cooldown reduction, making her even more effective in team fights. The build focuses on maximizing her damage output while ensuring she can sustain herself in prolonged engagements.

Key Equipment in the Second Build:

1. Boots (Dire Hit) - Before buying Sea Halberd, buy boots first with the Dire Hit blessing. This early purchase increases Masha's movement speed and crit damage, giving her better roaming potential and helping her engage more efficiently in early-game skirmishes.

2. Sea Halberd - Sea Halberd is the first core item. It provides physical penetration, crit chance, and lifesteal.This helps Masha sustain herself in fights, deal with tanky enemies, and remain effective in the early and mid-game.

3. Malefic Roar - The next item is Malefic Roar, which significantly increases physical penetration, allowing Masha to shred through enemies with high armor. This item is crucial for dealing with tanky heroes, especially in team fights.

4. Blade of the Heptaseas - Blade of the Heptaseas boosts Masha's damage against enemies with high HP. It offers physical attack and penetration, increasing her overall damage output, especially against tanky heroes.


5. Blade of Despair (BOD) - Blade of Despair gives Masha a massive power spike in physical attack, making her burst damage even more potent. It is particularly effective against low-health enemies, allowing Masha to quickly eliminate squishy targets.


6. Endless Battle - By replacing Hunter Strike with Endless Battle, Masha gains lifesteal, cooldown reduction, and attack damage. The extra movement speed and true damage proc from Endless Battle help her chase down enemies, sustain in fights, and deal consistent damage.

7. Rapid Boots - Increases her speed and crit damage for better mobility and late-game impact.

hero.hero_overview: Masha is a durable fighter with unique mechanics, making her a versatile pick in the roam role. She is known for her three HP bars, high sustain, and mobility, allowing her to thrive in initiating fights, split pushing, and tanking damage for her team. In the roam role, Masha focuses on providing vision, disrupting enemy farming routes, and peeling for her teammates during team fights. Her ability to destroy turrets quickly also makes her a threat in split-pushing strategies. With her high mobility and passive sustain, Masha excels in jungle invasions and can easily escape sticky situations.
hero.hero_strength:
Masha has excellent sustain with her passive, allowing her to stay in lane longer without needing to recall. However, burst and long-range heroes can be a challenge for her, as they can deal significant damage from a distance before she has the chance to engage or close the gap.


Her high mobility allows her to rotate quickly between lanes and jungle, making her a strong presence across the map.

With multiple HP bars, Masha is incredibly durable and can absorb a lot of damage, which makes her hard to take down in team fights.

She excels in jungle invasions, providing vision and stealing buffs, while also being a strong split-pusher who can create pressure on enemy turrets.

Masha can easily zone out squishy enemy heroes due to her ability to chase and lock them down.

Her unique strength lies in her ability to be a "Lockdown Chaser" she can finish and burst down enemies effectively.

Wild Power grants her enhanced speed, allowing her to quickly close the gap on fleeing enemies.

Her ultimate, Thunderclap, delivers burst damage and applies a hard lockdown, making her a strong threat in team fights and helping her secure high-priority targets.
hero.hero_weakness:
Laning/Farming Disadvantages:

Relatively weak wave clear compared to other roamers.

Dependent on HP to sustain, making her vulnerable to burst damage.


Team Fight Disadvantages:

Lacks reliable crowd control for locking down multiple enemies.

Can struggle to protect squishy allies due to limited utility skills.


Early/Mid/Late Game Disadvantages:

Early Game: Can be kited if the enemy has ranged damage or slows.

Mid Game: Relies heavily on her durability; improper positioning can lead to quick deaths.

Late Game: Vulnerable to burst damage that can deplete her HP bars quickly.


Unfavorable Hero Characteristics:

Struggles against heroes with strong burst damage or crowd control.

Requires good decision-making to maximize her utility in fights.

data.recommend: If you're looking to master Masha in Mobile Legends, this guide is perfect for you! It provides a detailed breakdown of her strengths, optimal strategies, and effective synergies with other heroes.

spell.spell_desc:
Flicker is a mobility spell that allows the hero to instantly dash a short distance in any direction. It can be used to quickly escape danger, reposition, or catch up to enemies.

Why Chose This Battle Spell for Masha:
Flicker is a great choice for Masha as it enhances her already strong mobility. It helps her close the gap quickly, especially when chasing down enemies or escaping from dangerous situations. Since Masha's role often involves engaging in team fights or split-pushing, Flicker adds an extra layer of versatility, allowing her to reposition if the situation turns unfavorable or secure crucial kills by catching up to fleeing enemies. 

Techniques and Tips:

Chasing: Use Flicker after Wold Power to quickly close the distance between you and an enemy hero, ensuring you can land your Thunderclap for a lockdown.

Escaping: If you're being overwhelmed or need to disengage from a fight, use Flicker to escape through walls or reposition to safety.

Engaging: Use Flicker to initiate fights, catching enemies off guard and allowing you to apply pressure before they can react.

Avoiding Skillshots: Flicker can be used to dodge incoming skillshots or abilities, making it an excellent tool for dodging burst damage or crowd control when you're trying to engage or retreat
